//: SHA-pinned Unity corpus for the Unity a11y-checker regression gate (#66/#69). Each entry is frozen at an exact commit so the only thing that can move the baseline numbers is THIS checker's own code (the Unity producer: src/unity-ast.ts L1, src/collect-unity.ts L3, src/unity-guid-registry.ts), never upstream repo drift. The producer emits no Findings yet (the structural-absence rules are children #70/#72/#73); the gated quantity here is per-asset PARSE OUTCOME — graph vs opaque(binary)/opaque(parse-error) — so Force-Text detection is a committed, drift-visible number (opaque is reported, not silently skipped — ADR 0004). To extend: add a public Unity game repo with real in-game UI, resolve its sha (`git ls-remote https://github.com/<owner>/<repo> HEAD`), append an entry, then re-bless with `pnpm unity:matrix:baseline`.

repos.source: "Chop Chop" — Unity-official open game, Unity-authored ground truth. Heavy real menu/HUD/inventory/dialogue UI, all uGUI prefabs (under UOP1_Project/Assets/Prefabs/UI/), text-serialized (%YAML 1.1) confirmed. The pinned sha is the main-branch HEAD at corpus seed (608eac98 == refs/heads/main), so a `--branch main --depth 1` clone parks exactly on it.
repos.caveat: uGUI ANCHOR ONLY, NOT a runtime-UI-Toolkit anchor. Its 4 .uxml files (Quests/Editor/StepDetail.uxml, Quests/Editor/DialogueLine.uxml, Quests/Resources/QuestEditorWindow.uxml, StateMachine/Editor/TransitionTableEditorWindow.uxml) are ALL Editor tooling, not runtime game UI — the in-game UI is 100% uGUI prefabs. So this repo does not exercise the runtime .uxml / UIDocument checker code path at all; a separate corpus repo using UI Toolkit for runtime UI is still needed to cover that path. Correction logged on #69.
